# Questions in Livestream 170

Live Stream #170: The Foundation of All Deposits

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=430" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:10</a> | Does *theopneustos* in 2 Timothy 3:16 mean inspired or life-giving? | He argues the context favors "God-breathed" or inspired more than a life-giving gloss. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=815" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:35</a> | In Jeremiah 13, why is the waistband not supposed to get wet? | He reads it as a parabolic act: the garment is preserved, hidden, and then ruined to symbolize Judah's corruption. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=1153" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:13</a> | What are your thoughts on baptismal regeneration and sacramental theology? | He is skeptical of making grace automatic through the rite itself and presses faith and allegiance to Christ as central. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=2126" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:26</a> | What is the strongest objection to reading the Merneptah Stele's Israel as biblical Israel? | He thinks the common objections are weak and still places that Israelite group in Canaan rather than somewhere else. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=2454" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:54</a> | If God is not omnipresent in the usual sense, how do you read passages like Jeremiah 23 and Psalm 139? | He distinguishes figurative language and divine glory from the stronger claim that God's full essence fills every place the same way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=3626" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:26</a> | What determined the canonicity of the historical and prophetic books in the Old Testament? | He distinguishes an informal Torah-centered canon from later formal criteria connected with Ezra-Nehemiah: credible authorship, temple reception, and true prophecy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=3941" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:41</a> | Would Joseph have been beaten after being falsely accused and arrested by Potiphar? | Yes. He says arrest, prison labor, and Egyptian punishment practices would likely have involved multiple beatings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=4152" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:12</a> | What does "sons of the prophets" mean in 2 Kings 2-3? | He identifies them with a school or guild of prophets where some were called as prophets and others served as support staff. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=4467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:27</a> | Is there a middle way between christocentric and christotelic readings of the Old Testament? | He says they are not mutually exclusive, but should be ordered: start with contextual exegesis and let that inform the christocentric reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=4793" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:53</a> | Is Luke 16:9 in the parable of the shrewd manager a sarcastic remark like Amos 4:4-5? | No. He reads it as a biting object lesson that the righteous should be wise and shrewd without losing innocence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=5387" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:47</a> | In 1 Peter 3:21, does baptism save us, and what is the appeal of a good conscience toward God? | He frames baptism as an antitype tied to salvation, while stressing that the passage is not about mere removal of dirt from the body. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=6156" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:36</a> | What is the biblical definition of witchcraft? | He distinguishes prayer from magic, defining magic as spellcraft and ritual, and witchcraft as magic tied to pagan deities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=6534" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:54</a> | How does biblical magic differ from magic in Harry Potter? | He says biblical or ancient ritual magic is usually directed toward a deity, while Harry Potter-style magic uses innate power amplified by spellcraft. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=8344" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:04</a> | Are Egyptian words in Exodus due to trade contact, escaped slaves, or Hyksos memory? | He rejects trade-route and Hyksos-memory explanations as sufficient, emphasizing that Exodus is an expulsion and that the route is not a main trade route. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=8688" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:48</a> | Is *The Complete Book of Enoch* by Jay Winter a good translation? | He says it seems decent and he has no major problem with it, but he has not closely compared the languages and is not an Enoch maximalist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=8777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:17</a> | Have you thought more about water and creation in ancient creation literature? | Yes. He says water is important across Genesis, Mesopotamian, and Egyptian creation traditions, with more going on than mere separation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=8883" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:03</a> | What do you mean by concordist theories of origins? | He defines concordism as trying to make biblical texts compatible with modern science, and argues that this can overload ancient religious texts with scientific expectations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=9313" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:13</a> | What is Paul trying to articulate in 2 Corinthians 12:1-7? | He reads the passage around Paul's visionary experience and says he can only give a limited answer from the text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=9929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:29</a> | What was the ancient meaning of the eye of Ra and Horus? | He treats them as distinct Egyptian divine-eye symbols and discusses them within Egyptian mythic and ritual categories. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=10090" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:10</a> | Are there Christian scholars you recommend for ancient Near Eastern or biblical background work? | He recommends Michael Heiser and John Walton as useful starting points. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=10892" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:32</a> | Is Jesus a failed apocalyptic prophet? | No. He rejects that framing and criticizes minimalist readings that treat Jesus' predictions as failed in a simplistic way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=11414" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:14</a> | Is Psalm 119 a good example of meditating on the law? | He says yes, connecting it to delight in and sustained reflection on God's instruction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=11770" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:10</a> | What are your thoughts on the flood story having dates while other ancient flood stories do not? | He connects that detail to Israelite chronology and says it supports a more historically framed presentation than many cognate flood accounts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=12079" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:19</a> | If you could go back in time and witness one biblical event, what would it be? | He treats the question playfully and discusses the limits of what such a choice could prove. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=12823" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:43</a> | What is the deal with holy numbers, and why are some numbers holier than others? | He says biblical number symbolism often comes from cultural patterns and repeated associations rather than intrinsic holiness in the number itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=13639" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:19</a> | Does Isaiah contradict Hebrew henotheism? | He argues that Isaiah's strong monotheistic language should not be flattened into a simplistic contradiction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=13759" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:19</a> | If the Kenite hypothesis is real, why would Yahweh be worshiped in Egypt? | He is skeptical of the hypothesis and says the evidence does not require Egyptian Yahweh worship in the proposed way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=14425" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:00:25</a> | What is your advice for people who keep struggling with specific sins despite prayer, reading, and fasting? | He says to keep repenting, keep siding with God against the sin, and keep falling back on Christ's grace. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=14626" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:46</a> | Are claims about the church being founded by Peter and Peter being buried in Rome historically reliable? | He says the church tradition is extensive, but he does not claim every detail can be proven from the transcript discussion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=14948" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:09:08</a> | How did ancient Egyptians do beekeeping? | He describes ceramic tube hives, smoking the front, and opening the back to harvest honey without destroying the hive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rlELX8XI7S8?t=15179" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:12:59</a> | What biblical themes should Christian fiction explore more often? | He calls for fiction that shows real darkness, real moral conflict, and grace that answers truthfully rather than sentimentalized piety. |
